data.description:

The announcement marks a major milestone for the cultural token platform, which has attracted over 33,000 users since its alpha […]

data.title: Differences Between Investing in Bitcoin and Its ETFs
data.description:
TL;DR Bitcoin ETFs simplify investing by shouldering operational responsibilities, while buying BTC outright grants full asset control and custody. BTC trades 24/7 on crypto exchanges with variable liquidity and trading fees; ETFs, however, trade during standard market hours and usually offer tighter spreads. Direct BTC purchases require investors to safeguard private keys and navigate uncertain.
source: https://crypto-economy.com/differences-between-investing-in-etfs-and-buying-btc-directly/

data.title: DePIN Ecosystem Startup dKloud Completes US$3.15 Million Strategic Round of Financing, with Animoca Brands and Others Participating
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: DePIN ecosystem startup dKloud announced the completion of a US$3.15 million strategic round of financing. Animoca Ventures, Blizzard Fund, Baboon.VC, Telos Foundation, TPS Capital and others under Animoca Brands participated in the investment. The new funds are intended to be used to build a decentralized cloud infrastructure platform to support the connection of DePIN with enterprise-level information technology (IT) to help companies deploy applications using cryptocurrencies and fiat currencies.

data.title: 2,200,000 People Affected As Data Breach Hits Retail Grocery Giant – Names, Government IDs, Bank Account Numbers and Health Info Stolen
data.description:
A massive data breach at a company connected to the grocery retail giant Ahold Delhaize USA impacted more than 2. 2 million victims. The breach targeted Ahold Delhaize USA Services, LLC, a firm that provides support services to Ahold Delhaize USA, one of the largest grocery retail groups on the East Coast
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2025/06/28/2200000-people-affected-as-data-breach-hits-retail-grocery-giant-names-government-ids-bank-account-numbers-and-health-info-stolen/

data.title: IDBot completed 2,000 transactions in 20 minutes in its second public beta test, and the third round of testing will start tonight
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Bitcoin Ecosystem Web3 Chat Group Robot IDBot recently completed the second round of public testing, completing more than 2,000 on-chain interactions within 20 minutes. This round of testing focuses on the "Crypto Red Packet" function. Users only need to enter simple commands in the group to participate in the lucky red packet distribution and experience the lightweight on-chain operation of "Web2 interface, Web3 interaction".
IDBot integrates multiple capabilities such as encrypted red packet distribution, on-chain content publishing, and digital asset management. Users can achieve complete Web3 interaction in a familiar social environment without jumping. As an independent application node based on MetaSo's deep customization, with its latest "secondary distribution" mechanism, users who publish content through IDBot will automatically participate in METASO incentive distribution and have the opportunity to obtain 90% of the reward share.
According to the official announcement, IDBot will hold the third round of red packet rain test activities at 21:00 (Hong Kong time) on June 28, with a total distribution amount of approximately 500 USDT of encrypted assets.

data.title: Trump's T1 phone removes the words "Made in the USA" and changes it to "Proudly Designed in the USA"
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The Trump Organization previously announced the launch of the Trump Mobile T1 and said it would use "Made in the USA" phones and services, priced at $499. However, the "Made in the USA" introduction on its reservation website seems to have been deleted, and replaced with a description of T1 with "American-Proud Design" and vague words such as "brought to life right here in the USA". It is reported that there is still no clear evidence that the T1 phone is truly made in the United States. Previously, many industry experts pointed out that the United States lacks a complete and mature smartphone manufacturing supply chain. Even if it can assemble finished products, it still needs to rely on a large number of overseas components.

data.title: Crypto scammer gets 8 years for $40M eEmpowerCoin, ECoinPlus scams
data.description:
Dwayne Golden sentenced to nearly eight years for defrauding investors in EmpowerCoin, ECoinPlus, and Jet-Coin schemes totaling over $40 million.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/crypto-scam-operator-gets-8-years-empowercoin-ecoinplus-jetcoin?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Crypto Ponzi Scheme Leader Sentenced to Nearly Eight Years in Prison
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Dwayne Golden, 57, has been sentenced to 97 months in prison for his involvement in a significant crypto Ponzi scheme. A federal judge in Brooklyn delivered the sentence on Friday, following Golden's conviction for wire fraud and money laundering. The Department of Justice (DOJ) revealed that Golden orchestrated scams through three digital asset firms—EmpowerCoin, ECoinPlus, and Jet-Coin—defrauding investors of over $40 million.

Federal prosecutors detailed how Golden and his associates promised investors guaranteed returns from crypto trading that never occurred. Instead, the funds were used to repay earlier investors or were misappropriated by the conspirators, a classic Ponzi scheme tactic. The companies collapsed soon after collecting investor deposits, leaving victims with substantial financial losses. The fraudulent activities took place between April and August 2017, with Golden and his partners falsely promoting their firms as international crypto traders.

Following the collapse of their companies, Golden and his co-defendants attempted to obstruct investigations by the Federal Trade Commission and a federal grand jury. Their efforts included destroying evidence and providing false information. United States Attorney Joseph Nocella described the scheme as an exploitation of investor enthusiasm for new technology, noting that the companies offered no legitimate services and conducted no actual cryptocurrency trading.

In addition to his prison sentence, Golden was ordered to forfeit approximately $2.46 million. Co-defendant William White received a 30-month sentence, while Gregory Aggesen and Marquis Egerton, also known as Mardy Eger, are awaiting sentencing. FBI Assistant Director Christopher Raia condemned the conspiracy as an elaborate scheme rooted in deceit and false promises, emphasizing Golden's disregard for integrity. Raia praised the sentence as a deterrent to potential scammers.

The DOJ has urged investors who suffered losses from the scheme to submit restitution claims through the FBI's dedicated portal. In a related incident earlier this month, five men pleaded guilty to orchestrating a $36.9 million crypto scam that defrauded Americans and funneled funds to a crypto scam center in Cambodia. The defendants targeted victims via social media, messaging apps, and dating platforms, enticing them with false promises of profitable crypto investments. So far in 2025, over $2.1 billion has been stolen in crypto-related incidents, with most losses attributed to wallet compromises and key mismanagement, according to CertiK co-founder Ronghui Gu.
